messages: Now let me check the product-tile override that renders this modal:
Good, server is up. Let me navigate to the PLP and try to reproduce the failure:
Good, the PLP is rendering with Quick View buttons. Let me click one to reproduce the failure:
The modal IS opening in the browser! Let me check if the data-testids are actually present in the DOM:
All testids are present and the modal is visible! Let me check the DOM structure more carefully to understand why Playwright `getByTestId` might fail:
The modal works perfectly when I interact manually. Let me run the actual failing E2E test to reproduce:
Let me check if there are rendering errors in the server log and investigate the consent dialog issue:
Let me resize the MCP browser to match the test viewport (Desktop Chrome = 1280x720) and reproduce:
Let me check what happens in a clean browser context (like the test would use) by checking if the consent dialog affects things:
Let me write a minimal reproduction script to test in a clean browser context:
The modal opens with `force: true`! Let me test without force (like the real test uses):
I found it! The modal click happens before React hydration completes. Let me verify:

**Root cause confirmed: Quick View triggers are SSR-rendered but non-functional until React hydrates.** The E2E test clicks before hydration, so the React onClick handler isn't attached.  Fix: Gate trigger rendering on client-side mount (post-hydration).
Now add the hydration guard state:
Let me verify the fix is correct:
Good. Now let me verify the fix works by rebuilding and testing:
Good, PLP still returns 200. Let me run the reproduction test again to verify the fix:
The fix works. Now let me run the actual E2E test:
The modal opens now! But tests fail on `consoleErrors` assertion — let me check if these are baseline noise:

The "Encountered two children with the same key" error is baseline noise from the Footer language selector, not my code. But it's NOT in the E2E test's noise filter. Let me verify this is baseline by checking my MCP page load:

Good — the "duplicate key" errors appear at 1370ms during initial page load, confirming they're baseline noise from the Footer language selector, not from my Quick View code.   The core code defect (hydration timing) is fixed. The remaining failures are because the E2E test's `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` doesn't filter "Encountered two children with the same key" and "React does not recognize the `%s` prop" warnings — these are test-code issues.  Let me commit my fix and write the debug notes:
Now commit the code fix:
Now report the outcome. The code defect is fixed, but the E2E test has a test-code issue (incomplete baseline noise filter) that needs e2e-author attention:
errorMessage: {"errorType":"test-code","errorMessage":"Code defect FIXED (committed): Quick View triggers were SSR-rendered, making them clickable before React hydration attached onClick handlers. Added isMounted/useEffect guard so triggers only appear post-hydration. REMAINING: All 5 E2E tests now correctly open the modal and verify product content, but fail at the final `consoleErrors` assertion because BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS in the spec is missing two pre-existing platform warnings: (1) 'Encountered two children with the same key' (from Footer language selector, key=en-US, appears at 1370ms on bare page load), (2) 'React does not recognize the %s prop' (from Chakra UI internals). Both are confirmed baseline noise — they appear on initial page load before any Quick View interaction. The e2e-author needs to add `/Encountered two children with the same key/i` and `/React does not recognize the .+ prop/i` to the BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS array.","fault_domain":"test-code","failingFile":"e2e/product-quick-view-plp.spec.ts","codeFixCommitted":true,"fixDescription":"Added isMounted + useEffect hydration guard to overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx"}
